items.title: Introduction
items.description: Welcome to Schema Weaver documentation. Learn about the platform and its core capabilities.
items.content:
Schema Weaver Documentation

Welcome to the official documentation for Schema Weaver. This guide will help you understand the architecture, core concepts, and how to start building amazing schemas and workflows.

What is Schema Weaver?

Schema Weaver is a modern, high-performance toolset designed to help you weave complex database schemas, manage data integrity, and visualize connections seamlessly.

Key Features

- Schema Visualization — Beautiful ER diagrams that auto-update as you evolve your
items.snippet: Welcome to the official documentation for Schema Weaver. This guide will help you understand the architecture, core concepts, and how to start building amazing schemas and workflows.

items.title: Configuration
items.description: Complete reference for schema-config.json options and environment variables
items.content:
Configuration

Schema Weaver is configured through a `schema-config.json` file in your project root, along with optional environment variables. This page is a complete reference for every available option.

Configuration File Location

Schema Weaver looks for config in the following order:

1. Path specified via `--config` flag
2. `./schema-config.json` in the current working directory
3. `~/.schema-weaver/config.json` (global user config)



---

Full Configuration Reference



---

`connection
items.snippet: Schema Weaver is configured through a `schema-config.json` file in your project root, along with optional environment variables. This page is a complete reference for every available option.

items.title: 5-Minute Quickstart
items.description: Get Schema Weaver running and apply your first migration in under 5 minutes
items.content:
5-Minute Quickstart

This guide takes you from zero to a working Schema Weaver setup with your first migration applied. You'll need a running PostgreSQL instance (or see [SQLite alternative](#using-sqlite-for-local-testing) below).

Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+ installed
- A PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQLite database
- npm or yarn

---

items.title: Drift Detection
items.description: Understand and manage schema drift in your databases
items.content:
Drift Detection

Drift detection helps you identify when your actual database schema differs from your version-controlled schema.

What is Schema Drift?

Schema drift occurs when your production database schema doesn't match what's defined in your migrations:



Common Causes

1. Hotfixes

Emergency SQL run directly in production without committing to version control:



2. Manual Changes

Team member modifies schema manually via GUI tools like pgAdmin.

3. Incomplete Migrations

Migration file
items.snippet: Drift detection helps you identify when your actual database schema differs from your version-controlled schema.

items.title: Core Concepts
items.description: Understand the fundamental concepts that power Schema Weaver
items.content:
Core Concepts

Learn the fundamental concepts that power Schema Weaver and enable safe, version-controlled database schema management.

What is a Schema?

A database schema is the structure that defines:

- Tables — Collections of related data organized in rows and columns
- Columns — Individual fields with specific data types and constraints
- Constraints — Rules that enforce data integrity (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK)
- Indexes — Performance optimizations for faster queries
- View
items.snippet: Learn the fundamental concepts that power Schema Weaver and enable safe, version-controlled database schema management.

items.title: Migrations
items.description: Master the migration system for safe, version-controlled schema changes
items.content:
Migrations

Master the Schema Weaver migration system for safe, version-controlled database changes.

What is a Migration?

A migration is a timestamped, version-controlled script that modifies your database schema.



Creating Migrations

Automatic Generation (Recommended)

Let Schema Weaver generate migrations from your schema definitions:



This inspects your database and generates the migration:



Manual Creation

For complex changes, write migrations manually:



Generates:

items.snippet: Master the Schema Weaver migration system for safe, version-controlled database changes.

items.title: Versioning
items.description: Manage schema versions and handle breaking changes
items.content:
Schema Versioning

Learn how to manage schema versions and handle breaking changes gracefully.

Version Your Schema

Semantic Versioning

Follow Semantic Versioning for your schema:



Examples



Breaking vs Non-Breaking Changes

Non-Breaking (MINOR version bump)

These are safe and don't require application changes:

✅ Add new column (with default or nullable)
✅ Add new table
✅ Add new index
✅ Create new view
✅ Add new constraint (if existing data satisfies it)



Breaking (MAJOR version bump)
items.snippet: Learn how to manage schema versions and handle breaking changes gracefully.

items.title: Authentication
items.description: API key and session token authentication for Schema Weaver REST API
items.content:
Authentication

Schema Weaver's REST API supports two authentication methods: API keys (for machine-to-machine access) and session tokens (for user-facing integrations). All API requests must include one of these.

---

API Keys

API keys are the recommended authentication method for:
- CI/CD pipelines
- Backend integrations
- Scripts and automation
- Third-party tool connections

Generating an API Key

1. Log in to [app.schema-weaver.io](https://app.schema-weaver.io)
2. Navigate to Settings → A
items.snippet: Schema Weaver's REST API supports two authentication methods: **API keys** (for machine-to-machine access) and **session tokens** (for user-facing integrations). All API requests must include one of t

items.title: API Reference
items.description: Complete API documentation for Schema Weaver
items.content:
API Reference

Complete documentation of Schema Weaver APIs.

Overview

Schema Weaver provides REST APIs for:

- Migrations — Create, preview, apply, and rollback migrations
- Schema — Inspect, diff, and validate schemas
- Connections — Manage database connections
- Snapshots — Access schema snapshots and history

All APIs require authentication via API key or session token.

Authentication

Include your API key in the `Authorization` header:



API Endpoints

Migrations

- `POST /v1/migrations`
items.snippet: Complete documentation of Schema Weaver APIs.

items.title: How Resona AI Works
items.description: Understand the agentic loop, tool execution pipeline, and the full set of analysis tools Resona uses to answer your data questions
items.content:
How Resona AI Works

Resona AI is not a simple chatbot. It is an agentic system — when you ask a question, it breaks the problem down into steps, calls specialized tools to gather and analyze data, and synthesizes the results into a complete answer. You can watch every step happen in real time.

![Resona AI agentic interface showing the planning and execution phases](/images/data-explorer/resona-ai.png)

---

The Three Phases

Every Resona response goes through three phases, visible in the Agent

items.title: Charts & Analysis Results
items.description: Understand the artifact types Resona AI generates — charts, stat blocks, and data tables — and how to interact with them
items.content:
Charts & Analysis Results

When Resona AI finishes an analysis, it outputs artifacts — rich interactive components embedded directly in the chat. These are not static images. You can expand them, sort them, pin them to a dashboard, and export their underlying SQL.

![Resona AI chat response with an expanded interactive data table artifact](/images/data-explorer/resoan-ai-chat-expand-table.png)

---

Data Tables

When Resona runs a SQL query or returns row-level results, it renders them as an int

items.title: AI Chat Interface
items.description: Use Resona AI inside the Data Explorer to query, analyze, and visualize your data using natural language
items.content:
AI Chat Interface

Resona AI is the agentic assistant built directly into the Data Explorer. You interact with it through a chat panel — type a question in plain English, and Resona queries your database, runs analysis tools, and returns results as interactive charts, tables, and reports.

![Resona AI chat panel showing a table mention with table overview context](/images/data-explorer/resona-ai-@mention-table-overview.png)

Opening the AI Panel

Click the Resona AI button in the Data Explorer s
items.snippet: Resona AI is the agentic assistant built directly into the Data Explorer. You interact with it through a chat panel — type a question in plain English, and Resona queries your database, runs analysis

items.title: Resona AI
items.description: Overview of Resona AI inside the Data Explorer — what it can do, how to access it, and where to go next
items.content:
Resona AI

Resona AI is the agentic data assistant built into the Data Explorer. You describe what you want to know in plain English — Resona figures out which tables to query, runs the analysis, and returns the results as interactive charts, tables, and reports.

![Resona AI panel open inside the Data Explorer with an active conversation and chart artifact](/images/data-explorer/resona-ai.png)

---

What Resona Can Do

Explore and query your data

Ask questions without writing SQL. Resona autom

items.title: Workspace & Projects
items.description: Manage generated reports, organize analysis into projects, and browse your full artifact history in the Data Explorer
items.content:
Workspace & Projects

Resona AI stores everything it generates. The Workspace holds exported files, Projects organize your analysis sessions, and Artifact History gives you a searchable archive of every chart and result ever produced.

![Resona AI interface showing the workspace and project organization panel](/images/data-explorer/resona-ai.png)

---

Workspace

The Workspace is a persistent file store for reports and exports generated by Resona. It is accessible from the `W` shortcut or the wo
items.snippet: Resona AI stores everything it generates. The **Workspace** holds exported files, **Projects** organize your analysis sessions, and **Artifact History** gives you a searchable archive of every chart a

items.title: Exploring Your Data
items.description: Browse tables, inspect columns, filter rows, and analyze column statistics in the Data Explorer grid
items.content:
Exploring Your Data

Once you've [connected a database](/docs/data-explorer/connecting-database), the Data Explorer gives you a two-panel interface for browsing live data — a sidebar of tables on the left and a high-performance data grid on the right.

---

Interface Layout

![The Data Explorer showing the two-column layout with left sidebar and main data grid](/images/data-explorer/data-explorer.png)

| Region | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| Left sidebar | Schema selector, table search, tabl

items.title: Exporting Data
items.description: Export individual tables or entire database schemas to CSV, JSON, NDJSON, Excel, and SQL formats
items.content:
Exporting Data

The Data Explorer lets you export data in multiple formats — either a single table or your entire database schema as a ZIP archive. Exports are triggered directly from the browser and download instantly.

---

Export Button

![The Export button and format selection dropdown in the Data Explorer action bar](/images/data-explorer/export.png)

The Export button appears in the top-right action bar when you have a connection selected. Click it to open a dropdown with two sections:

1.

items.title: Data Explorer
items.description: Query your databases interactively with the Schema Weaver Data Explorer
items.content:
Data Explorer

The Data Explorer is a built-in SQL workbench that lets you connect to your databases, run queries, and explore your data — all inside Schema Weaver. It integrates with Resona AI so you can ask questions in plain English and get runnable SQL back instantly.

What You Can Do

- Browse live data — Two-panel layout with a table sidebar and a high-performance data grid
- Smart column sizing — Canvas-measured, 95th-percentile column widths that auto-fit your data
- Filter & sort — Serv
items.snippet: The Data Explorer is a built-in SQL workbench that lets you connect to your databases, run queries, and explore your data — all inside Schema Weaver. It integrates with Resona AI so you can ask questi

items.title: AI Chat
items.description: Use Resona AI Chat to query your database with natural language
items.content:
AI Chat

Resona AI Chat lets you have a back-and-forth conversation with your database. Ask questions in plain English, refine your results, and build up complex analyses step by step — without writing a single line of SQL.

---

Opening AI Chat

From the Data Explorer

Click the AI Assist button in the top-right of the query editor, or press `Ctrl+Shift+A`.

Standalone Chat Panel

Click Resona AI in the main navigation sidebar to open the full-width chat interface.

Keyboard Shortcut

From anyw
items.snippet: Resona AI Chat lets you have a back-and-forth conversation with your database. Ask questions in plain English, refine your results, and build up complex analyses step by step — without writing a singl

items.title: Resona AI
items.description: Meet Resona, the AI-powered data assistant built into Schema Weaver
items.content:
Resona AI

Resona is Schema Weaver's built-in AI data assistant. It understands your database schema and lets you explore, query, and analyze your data using natural language — no SQL expertise required.

What Resona Can Do

Natural Language to SQL

Ask questions about your data in plain English. Resona generates optimized SQL, explains it step by step, and runs it for you:

> "How many new users signed up each week over the last 3 months?"



Automatic Chart Generation

When results contain num
items.snippet: Resona is Schema Weaver's built-in AI data assistant. It understands your database schema and lets you explore, query, and analyze your data using natural language — no SQL expertise required.

items.title: Models and Credits
items.description: Available AI models in Resona, credit costs, and plan limits
items.content:
Models and Credits

Resona AI supports multiple large language models so you can balance speed, quality, and cost. Each query consumes credits from your monthly allowance based on which model you use.

---

Available Models

| Model | Provider | Credits | Context Window | Best For |
|-------|----------|---------|----------------|----------|
| Free model | Schema Weaver | 0 | 8k tokens | Basic lookups, simple queries |
| GPT-4o mini | OpenAI | 1x | 128k tokens | Everyday queries, fast iteration |
items.snippet: Resona AI supports multiple large language models so you can balance speed, quality, and cost. Each query consumes credits from your monthly allowance based on which model you use.

items.title: Schema Visualization
items.description: Explore and understand your database structure with interactive ER diagrams
items.content:
Schema Visualization

Schema Weaver automatically generates interactive Entity-Relationship (ER) diagrams from your live database. Explore tables, relationships, and constraints visually — no manual diagramming required.

---

Opening the ER Diagram

Cloud Platform

Navigate to your project and click Schema in the top navigation. The ER diagram loads automatically from your connected database.

CLI (Local Web UI)



The diagram renders in seconds for most schemas. For very large schemas (500+ ta
items.snippet: Schema Weaver automatically generates interactive Entity-Relationship (ER) diagrams from your live database. Explore tables, relationships, and constraints visually — no manual diagramming required.

items.title: Migrations
items.description: Deep dive into Schema Weaver's Atlas-powered migration engine — CI/CD, rollbacks, and advanced patterns

headings.title: How Schema Weaver's Migration Engine Works
Adding a Not-Null Column with a Default

items.content:
Migrations Deep Dive

This guide goes beyond the basics to cover production-grade migration workflows: CI/CD integration, zero-downtime patterns, multi-environment management, conflict resolution, and the Atlas engine that powers Schema Weaver's DDL safety checks.

> New to migrations? Start with [Core Concepts: Migrations](/docs/core-concepts/migrations) for the fundamentals, then come back here.

---
